agent_reply: Implemented `lib/BluetoothManager` from the README.
What’s in place:
- `BtManager` namespace with the requested state machine.
- HFP client init and callbacks.
- Connect by MAC, with a bonded-device check that returns `NotBonded`.
- Pairing mode and incoming-connection waiting mode.
- Disconnect that also ends pairing/waiting/connection attempts.
- Pairing success callback with MAC + name, then immediate disconnect while in pairing mode.
- Audio callback registration for incoming/outgoing HFP audio.
- `pickupPhone()` via HFP answer-call.
- State/result names for logging/UI use.
Verification:
- I added a temporary compile harness so PlatformIO would actually build `lib/BluetoothManager`, and that passed.
- I removed the harness afterward and ran the normal `pio run`; that also passed.
- One caveat: because nothing in `src` currently includes `BluetoothManager.h`, PlatformIO won’t link it into the final firmware until a real module starts using it.

Strategy note: your plan makes sense overall. The only part I’d want to test on hardware is “waiting for incoming connection”, because HFP client/server roles and phone behavior can be picky there. The implementation matches the existing pairing demo style: make the ESP32 connectable/discoverable, then let the HFP callbacks drive state.

user_prompt: make the new BluetoothManager handle this, by calling into AudioManager
agent_reply: Done in [BluetoothManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:257).
`ESP_HF_CLIENT_VOLUME_CONTROL_EVT` now handles speaker volume by mapping the HFP `0..15` value into `AudioManager`’s `0..10` range and calling:
```cpp
AudioManager::setVolume(audio_volume);
I left microphone gain requests as a log-only path for now, because `AudioManager` currently has mic mute but no real mic gain control.
Verification:
- Built once with a temporary compile hook so `BluetoothManager` was actually compiled: passed.
- Removed the hook and ran normal `pio run`: passed.
- Existing third-party/unrelated warnings remain.

user_prompt: hmmm this slipped by me, AudioManager should not be applying the volume to samples, it should just set the volume on the amplifier or DAC, if you don't have enough data on how to do this then just put in a stub, remove both `mono_to_stereo_with_volume` and `apply_volume`
also, make the volume range handled by AudioManager 0 to 30 so we can handle bluetooth 0 to 15 by steps of 2 and generic 10% increments with steps of 3
agent_reply: Done.

AudioManager now uses a `0..30` volume range in [AudioManager.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.h:28). `volumeUp()`/`volumeDown()` step by `3`, and Bluetooth HFP volume maps `0..15` directly to `0..30` by multiplying by `2`.

I removed sample-volume scaling from [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:289). The speaker pump now uses `AudioFifo::dequeueStereo()` directly, so samples are duplicated mono-to-stereo but not gain-adjusted. The old `apply_volume` and `mono_to_stereo_with_volume` helpers are gone.
Hardware volume is now an explicit stub: `update_hardware_volume()`. It just logs for now, which keeps the control path in the right place without pretending we know the Core2 amp/DAC gain route yet.
Verification:
- Built with a temporary BluetoothManager compile hook: passed.
- Removed the hook and ran normal `pio run`: passed.
- Remaining warnings are the existing unrelated third-party / packed-member warnings.

user_prompt: for BluetoothManager, inside the handler for `ESP_HF_CLIENT_AUDIO_STATE_EVT` , I don't see the same handling for `ESP_HF_CLIENT_AUDIO_STATE_CONNECTED`, `ESP_HF_CLIENT_AUDIO_STATE_CONNECTED_MSBC`, I only see the `ESP_HF_CLIENT_AUDIO_STATE_DISCONNECTED` state being handled.

AudioManager needs to accept a setting to know if mSBC decoding is required or not, and if the samples are 8 kHz or 16 kHz so it can pick the right way to queue into the FIFO. BluetoothManager needs to use this feature.
agent_reply: Implemented.
BluetoothManager now handles all three HFP audio states in [BluetoothManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/BluetoothManager/BluetoothManager.cpp:250):
- `ESP_HF_CLIENT_AUDIO_STATE_CONNECTED` -> `AudioManager::setHfpAudioFormat(Cvsd, 8000)`
- `ESP_HF_CLIENT_AUDIO_STATE_CONNECTED_MSBC` -> `AudioManager::setHfpAudioFormat(Msbc, 16000)`
- `ESP_HF_CLIENT_AUDIO_STATE_DISCONNECTED` -> resets to CVSD/8 kHz before reconnect attempt
AudioManager now has a public HFP audio format setting in [AudioManager.h](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.h:26):
```cpp
enum class HfpCodec { Cvsd, Msbc };
bool setHfpAudioFormat(HfpCodec codec, uint32_t sampleRateHz);
HfpCodec hfpAudioCodec();
uint32_t hfpAudioSampleRateHz();
And the callbacks now use that setting:
- Incoming CVSD queues PCM as 8 kHz so `AudioFifo` upsamples.
- Incoming mSBC decodes with `libsbc`, then queues decoded 16 kHz PCM.
- Outgoing CVSD downsamples internal 16 kHz mic FIFO to 8 kHz.
- Outgoing mSBC encodes 16 kHz PCM with `libsbc`.
Verification:
- Built with temporary BluetoothManager compile hook: passed.
- Removed hook and ran normal `pio run`: passed.
- `clang-format` was not available on PATH, so I did not run formatting. Existing unrelated warnings remain.
